mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hazard ratio for negative returns after the threshold breach falls below 1.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real GDP growth indicate a supply-demand mismatch where falling output outpaces demand contraction, compressing profit margins and driving equity returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint cross-sectional rank of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX direction with an IC greater than 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not diverge significantly between high and low interaction quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rapid increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor inflation expectations, causing real rates to spike and equity multiples to contract sharply over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not decline significantly when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate hiring, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns show no directional bias following consecutive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million annualized units, it signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances following the housing starts threshold breach, or the predictive power of HOUST drops below a 0.02 Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent spikes in unemployment and core inflation indicate a wage-price spiral that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The cross-moment of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL changes fails to explain more than 1 percent of the variance in 20-day forward SPX returns, or forward returns do not systematically decline when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a sudden surge in real GDP growth triggers supply bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, resulting in negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not decline when GDPC1 growth accelerates alongside high INDPRO levels, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample testing.
